# unitforge

> A units-of-measure library for arbitrary domains. Three primitives (`forge`, `defineUnit`, `defineConversion`) work against any dimension and unit a consumer (or kit author) declares. First-class custom dimensions for non-physical domains (game state, business metrics, simulation, packaging, factions); per-unit tree-shake; works as well for one Coke = N donuts as for SI units.

unitforge is a TypeScript unit conversion library built around atomic named exports. It has no global registry, no bundled lookup table, and no fluent string-dispatch API. Your import graph is your runtime conversion graph, so production bundles include only the units and conversions you import.

## Status

Published to npm. Public API is stable. The shipped runtime surface is `forge`, `defineUnit`, `defineConversion`, `linear`, `ValidationError`, `DEFAULT_MEMO_CAP`, and `MEMO_CAP_MAX`; types are `Dimension`, `Unit`, `Conversion`, `ForgeInput`, `ForgeOutput`, `UnitMap`, `ValidatorMap`, and `ValidationFailure`.

## Thesis

Units, dimensions, and conversions are values you import. Within-dimension conversions are free; cross-dimensional conversions are explicit, single-hop, and registered via `defineConversion`. Custom dimensions and units are first-class, the same shape as the library's own kits.

## API surface

- `forge(from, to, config?)`: returns a converter function. Three overloads cover within-dim (scalar in, scalar out, no `via`); cross-dim with scalar output (object in, scalar out, `via` required); cross-dim with object output (object in, object out, `via` required). `NoInfer<D>` on the within-dim `to` parameter makes mismatched-dimension forges a compile error.
- `defineUnit(spec)`: returns a `Unit`. Spec is `{ id, label, symbol, dimension, toBase, fromBase, base? }`. `id` is a stable kebab-case identifier; `label` is the human display name (`'Square Meter'`); `symbol` is the conventional short form (`'m²'`). `safeCopy` rejects prototype-pollution keys at definition time.
- `defineConversion(spec)`: returns a `Conversion`. Spec is `{ inputs, output, validate?, compute }`. `compute` runs in base units; the library normalizes inputs from whatever unit the call site uses before calling it.
- `linear(scale)`: helper returning `{ toBase, fromBase }` for the linear case. For userland one-offs only; kit-shipped units MUST inline closures because the spread `...linear(scale)` introduces a `CallExpression` inside the spec literal which defeats per-export tree-shaking even when `defineUnit(...)` is `/*#__PURE__*/`-marked.

## Kits

**Foundational kits** (one dimension each; canonical atoms; domain kits re-export from these):

- `unitforge/kits/length`: LENGTH units. SI (meter base, mm, cm, dm, km, µm, nm) + customary (in, ft, yd, mi, fathom, mil) + astronomical (au, ly, parsec) + nautical (nautical mile) + crystallographic (ångström).
- `unitforge/kits/volume`: VOLUME units. SI (cubic meter base, cubic mm/cm/dm/km, mL, cL, dL, L) + cubic-imperial (cubic in/ft/yd) + fluid-ounce variants (US, UK) + spoons (tsp/tbsp in US, UK, metric, AU) + cup variants (US Customary, US Legal FDA, UK Imperial, metric 250 mL, Japanese rice gō 180.39 mL, Japanese general 200 mL, Russian stakan 250 mL).
- `unitforge/kits/mass`: MASS units. SI (kilogram base, gram, milligram, microgram, tonne) + US customary (ounce avoirdupois, pound, stone, short ton, long ton) + Asian regional (jin PRC 500 g via GB/T 17710-1999, jin HK/Taiwan/Singapore 600 g).
- `unitforge/kits/temperature`: TEMPERATURE units. Kelvin (base, SI) + Celsius + Fahrenheit + Rankine. First kit with AFFINE conversions; the values-vs-deltas posture is values-only for v1 (deltas are consumer concern; see issue #30).

**Domain kits** (re-export atoms from foundational kits; add domain-specific surface):

- `unitforge/kits/geometry`: re-exports LENGTH atoms from `kits/length` and VOLUME atoms from `kits/volume`; defines its own AREA (square meter base, square mm/cm/km/in/ft/yd/mi, acre, hectare, are) and ANGLE (radian base, degree, gradian, arcminute, arcsecond, turn) units. Adds 40+ shape derivations: rectangle, square, triangle (base+height, Heron's, equilateral), trapezoid, parallelogram, rhombus, kite, ellipse, annulus, sector, segment, regular polygon, cuboid, cube, sphere, cylinder area / volume / perimeter; `cartesianFromPolar` / `polarFromCartesian` (cross-dim point coordinates); `midpointBetweenPoints` (within-dim).
- `unitforge/kits/data-storage`: DATA units; decimal bytes (kilobyte through petabyte, ×1000), binary bytes (kibibyte through pebibyte, ×1024 per IEC 80000-13), bits (bit through gigabit, 1 bit = 1/8 byte). Currently foundational-shaped (no re-exports) since DATA only appears in this kit.
- `unitforge/kits/cooking`: re-exports VOLUME atoms from `kits/volume`, MASS atoms from `kits/mass`, and TEMPERATURE atoms from `kits/temperature`; defines its own cooking-tradition packaging units (stick of butter, EU butter block 250 g volumetric, dash, pinch) and cooking-tradition temperature descriptors (lowHeat, mediumLowHeat, mediumHeat, mediumHighHeat, highHeat; each value-1 maps to a fixed °F midpoint). Demo includes a soda-vs-food sugar comparator using a userland custom `'sugar'` dimension (one Coke ≈ 9.75 sugar cubes; one Coke ≈ 3.9 glazed donuts).

**JS-identity invariant.** `cooking.milliliter`, `geometry.milliliter`, and `volume.milliliter` resolve to the SAME `Unit` JS object. `forge(cooking.milliliter, geometry.milliliter)` is identity, not a converter through base. Same for every cross-kit re-export; verified by `test/kits/cross-kit-identity.test.ts`.

## Forge pipeline (cross-dim)

Every cross-dim `forge(...)` invocation runs this ordering:

1. Defensive copy of input via `safeCopy` (rejects prototype-pollution keys `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`).
2. Cache check if `memoize > 0`. Cache key includes precision-rounded input values when `precision` is set.
3. Run every validator on the RAW input (as the caller supplied it, in the caller's chosen units; NOT base-normalized): per-property validators from `via.validate` and the call-site `validate?` config, plus the optional `_all` validator that runs on the destructured raw-input object as a whole. `_all` runs regardless of per-property outcomes; failures accumulate into a single `ValidationError`.
4. Throw `ValidationError` if any validator failed. Each `ValidationFailure` includes the original `Error` (if a validator threw) on `failure.cause`.
5. Normalize each input from its passed-in unit to base units via the unit's `toBase`.
6. Run `compute` in base units.
7. Denormalize outputs via the target unit(s) `fromBase`. Supports both single-unit and multi-unit (object-output) destinations.
8. Apply `precision` rounding to the output if set.
9. Write to cache if `memoize > 0`.
10. Return the result.

Within-dim forges skip the conversion lookup, the validator gauntlet, and the input-object marshalling. They reduce to `to.fromBase(from.toBase(value))` with optional precision rounding and memoization.

## Validator contract

A validator receives the input value AS THE CALLER SUPPLIED IT (in the caller's chosen unit, not base-normalized) for its key (per-property), or the destructured raw-input object for `_all`. Return convention:

- `true` or `undefined`: pass.
- `string`: reject with that string as the failure message.
- Throw: caught and recorded as a `ValidationFailure` with the original `Error` preserved on `failure.cause`.

All validators run on every call; failures aggregate into a single `ValidationError` thrown after the entire gauntlet completes. Validators MUST be pure functions of their input. They are skipped on cache hits.

## Dimension type and the `(string & {})` brand

`Dimension` is declared as `(typeof DIMENSIONS)[number] | (string & {})`. The `(string & {})` half is a known TypeScript trick: it's structurally identical to `string` but the union doesn't collapse to plain `string` during inference. Practical effect:

- Built-in dimensions (`'length'`, `'area'`, `'volume'`, `'data'`, `'angle'`) surface in autocomplete.
- User-defined dimensions (`'gold' as const`, `'arpygee/coin' as const`, etc.) are accepted without TypeScript complaining about a missing union member.
- A passed string literal that doesn't match a built-in name is treated as a user-defined dimension, not an error.

This is how custom dimensions are first-class without a registration step.

## Custom-dimension prefix convention

When you ship a kit publicly (a published package, an org-wide internal kit, anything that might be imported alongside another third-party kit), prefix dimension strings with your package or namespace:

```ts
const COIN = '@mycorp/coin' as const;  // or 'mycorp/coin', 'arpygee/coin', etc.
```

Rationale: two kits both exporting `const MANA = 'mana' as const` would silently forge against each other. A consumer importing both could write `forge(magicSchool.mana, gpu.mana)` and the library would happily build a converter. Dimension strings are trusted opaque values; the library does not enforce uniqueness.

The convention lives at the kit-author tier. Bare strings (`'count'`, `'length'`) are fine in single-app userland code and in pedagogical examples. They become a foot-gun the moment your code ships under an npm package name.

The `arpy-gee-shop` demo (`demo/src/components/kits/forge/sections/arpy-gee-shop.tsx`) uses `'arpygee/coin'` and `'arpygee/goods'` as a worked example.

## Subpath imports

| Subpath | Exports |
| --- | --- |
| `unitforge` | API barrel: `forge`, `defineUnit`, `defineConversion`, `linear`, `ValidationError`, `DEFAULT_MEMO_CAP`, `MEMO_CAP_MAX`, plus types |
| `unitforge/dimensions` | `LENGTH`, `AREA`, `VOLUME`, `DATA`, `ANGLE`, `DIMENSIONS` tuple, `Dimension` type |
| `unitforge/kits/<kit>` | every unit and conversion shipped by `<kit>` |
| `unitforge/validation` | `ValidationError`, `ValidationFailure` type |
| `unitforge/version` | `VERSION: string` (read from `package.json` at runtime; on its own subpath because the JSON import would inline `package.json` into every consumer bundle) |

## Tree-shake implementation

- `"sideEffects": false` in `package.json`.
- Every kit unit and conversion is `/*#__PURE__*/`-marked so importing only `meter` drops `centimeter`, `squareMeter`, and every other unused export.
- All exports are named; no default export.
- Per-kit subpath (`unitforge/kits/<name>`) so importing from `geometry` never pulls a sibling kit.
- Kit unit definitions inline `toBase`/`fromBase` closures rather than spreading `...linear(scale)` because a `CallExpression` inside the spec literal defeats per-export tree-shake even when the outer `defineUnit(...)` is PURE-marked.

## Kit categories

Three categories of kit; each has different authoring rules. Decide which a new kit is before opening the directory.

1. **Foundational**: canonical units in a well-known dimension; one dimension per kit. Today: `length` (LENGTH), `volume` (VOLUME), `mass` (MASS), `temperature` (TEMPERATURE), `data-storage` (DATA). Future: `area` and `angle` (currently in geometry; promote when a second domain kit needs them), `time`, `electricity`, `hydro/flow`, `light`, `force-and-energy`, `velocity`, `currency`. Must be canon-correct (NIST / IEC / ISO / BIPM / IEEE values), comprehensive within the dimension, and JSDoc-cite the standard. Reviewer pairing: domain expert (geometer / pharmacist / data-hoarder / mrp-planner / astrometrist / antiquary / color-scientist / gastronomist) plus architect for surface coherence.
2. **Composition / domain**: curated barrel for a working domain. Re-exports atomic units from one or more foundational kits + adds domain-specific units and conversions. Today: `cooking` (VOLUME + MASS + TEMPERATURE re-exports + cooking-tradition packaging + heat descriptors), `geometry` (LENGTH + VOLUME re-exports + AREA + ANGLE atoms + shape derivations). Future (tracked as issue #37): `home-construction` (the proof-test for the pattern; cherry-picks from LENGTH + MASS + VOLUME + TEMPERATURE + future ELECTRICITY + HYDRO + LIGHT). **The re-exported `Unit` instance is the same JS object as the foundational source**; `forge(cooking.milliliter, volume.milliliter)` is identity. Verified by `test/kits/cross-kit-identity.test.ts`. Never redefine a unit a foundational kit already ships. Reviewer pairing: working-profession expert plus architect.
3. **In-universe**: invented dimensions, mostly self-contained. Example: the ArPeeGee shop demo (`demo/src/components/kits/forge/sections/arpy-gee-shop.tsx`) ships `arpygee/coin` and `arpygee/goods` dimensions with silver / gold / shield units. Future: `battletech` (TONNAGE / DAMAGE / HEAT / MP / BV / C-BILLS), `dnd-currency`, `sci-fi-power-levels`, `magic-mana`. Prefix dimension names with the universe to avoid collision (`battletech/heat`, not `heat`). Cite source material (rulebook edition, record sheets). Reviewer pairing: genre lens (e.g., battletech-grognard).

Cross-kit imports between foundational and composition kits are explicitly blessed and tree-shake-correct. Every export is `/*#__PURE__*/` and named, so a composition kit's barrel pays the byte cost only of the exports it actually re-exports. Domain composition is a curation problem, not an engineering one.

## Extending the library

EXTENDING.md is canonical. Distilled rules below; check the doc for worked examples.

**Lib-side (new kit at `src/kits/<kit>/`):**

- Three files: `units.ts`, `conversions.ts` (if any cross-dim), `index.ts` barrel (`export * from './units.js'; export * from './conversions.js';`).
- Every `defineUnit({...})` and `defineConversion({...})` MUST be `/*#__PURE__*/`-marked. Spec literal MUST NOT contain any `CallExpression`; specifically, do not spread `...linear(scale)` inside a kit's `defineUnit` spec. Use inline `toBase: (v) => v * k` / `fromBase: (b) => b / k` closures. The `linear()` helper is exported for userland one-offs only.
- Unit identity is three readonly strings: `id` (stable kebab-case, e.g. `'square-meter'`), `label` (title-case display, e.g. `'Square Meter'`), `symbol` (conventional short form, e.g. `'m²'`). JS export binding convention: kebab `id` → camelCase identifier (`square-meter` → `squareMeter`). Never rename `id` after release; it is part of the public API.
- `base: true` marks the canonical unit (`toBase`/`fromBase` are identity). Exactly one unit per dimension may set it; the library does not enforce uniqueness at compile time, so the test suite is the safety net.
- New dimensions go in `src/dimensions.ts` as `export const X = 'x' as const;` and must be appended to the `DIMENSIONS` tuple in the same file. The string value is part of the public API.
- Cross-kit conversions (rare): co-locate with the more derived kit's units, not a shared `conversions/` directory; preserves subpath tree-shake.
- `defineUnit` / `defineConversion` route specs through `safeCopy`, which throws on the reserved keys `__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`. Do not pick these as `id`s.
- `package.json`: the `./kits/*` wildcard export covers the new subpath automatically; no edit required.
- Tests: per-kit at `test/kits/<kit>.test.ts`. Assert per unit: id/label/symbol/dimension match docs; `toBase(known)` value; `fromBase(toBase(x)) === x` round-trip; `base: true` only on the canonical unit. Per conversion: `compute` correctness, `validate` rejection messages, and a `forge(...)` end-to-end across more than one input unit (proves normalization).
- Update llms.txt's `## Kits` list when adding a kit.

**Demo-side (new interactive surface at `demo/src/components/kits/<kit>/`):**

- Three-file registration: kit directory itself, append entry to `demo/src/components/kits/registry.ts` (`KITS` tuple), append `'<kit>-dark'` + `'<kit>-light'` recipes to `demo/src/components/theme/recipes.ts` (extend `KitId` union). Both theme variants are mandatory.
- Kit directory contents: `index.tsx` (Screen + `meta: KitMeta` export), `units.ts` (re-export lib units as ordered arrays for picker iteration, e.g. `LENGTH_UNITS`), `<kit>.css` (data-theme blocks for dark + light), `parts/<kit>-backdrop.tsx`, `sections/<section>.tsx` per section.
- `KitMeta` shape: `{ id, label, blurb, defaultThemeId, icon (LucideIcon), previewBg? }`. `id` is the route segment (`#/<id>`).
- Theme CSS variables to set in each `[data-theme='<kit>-<variant>']` block: `--uf-bg`, `--uf-fg`, `--uf-muted`, `--uf-accent`, `--uf-border`, `--uf-card`, `--uf-code-bg`. Optionally kit-specific decorative vars (`--uf-trace`, `--uf-trace-faint`, `--uf-grid`, `--uf-grid-faint`).
- Screen owns `BenchState = { fromId, toId, value }`. Seed `fromId` / `toId` MUST be `id`s present in the unit catalog passed to `Bench`'s `options`; `findById` throws on miss.
- Screen composes its page chrome via `<KitLayout>` (imported from `../layout.js`), passing `backdropZone`, `headerZone`, optional `benchZone`, `sectionsZone`, optional `footerZone`. `KitLayout` is purely positional; the Screen decides everything.
- Sections compose `<SectionLayout headerZone introZone widgetZone={<WidgetLayout interactionZone codeZone />}>`. Section-authoring primitives in `demo/src/components/ui/`: `UnitPicker` (takes `units: ReadonlyArray<{id, label, symbol}>`), `Slider`, `Result` (variant `'standard' | 'hero'`), `CodeBlock`. Prefer these over hand-rolled markup. Named Organ extraction: a section file's widget body past ~50 lines (or carrying a self-contained visual artifact with bounded prop surface, cadence mismatch, sink-not-source) extracts as a sibling component in the same file; organs are sink-only and never take chassis flags (`selected`, `loading`, `disabled`).

## Testing

Three layers, every layer gated on every CI build.

**Example-based tests** (`bun test`): 470+ tests across 16 files; ~18.5k `expect()` assertions (the fast-check fuzz tests randomize their assertion count per run, so this number drifts). Covers `forge`, `defineUnit`, `defineConversion`, dimensions, every shipped kit, the `lib/` primitives (`math`, `memoize`, `safeCopy`, `validation`), and demo-side cross-file invariants (see `test/demo-invariants.test.ts`). 100.00% line coverage; 98.42% function coverage (the gap is unit-definition factor closures in `kits/geometry/units.ts` and `kits/cooking/units.ts` exercised transitively through `forge` rather than directly). Tracked via Codecov.

**Property-based fuzz** (`test/fuzz/`, 6 files, fast-check): within-dim round-trip + composition, cross-dim monotonicity, precision-0 integer output guarantee, `ValidationError` invariants, memoize cap-invariance, and `defineUnit` prototype-pollution hygiene. Also satisfies the OpenSSF Scorecard Fuzzing check.

**Mutation testing** (Stryker, `bun run mutation`) over the `forge` core, the `define` API (`defineUnit` + `defineConversion`), and four `lib/` primitives (`math`, `memoize`, `safeCopy`, `validation`).

- CI break threshold: 75%.
- Current score: 96.36%; 238 mutants killed, 9 classified equivalent under the public API, 0 timeouts, 0 errors.
- Per file: `math` / `define` / `safeCopy` / `validation` at 100%; `memoize` at 95.74%; `forge` at 92.55%.

Coverage measures whether the suite *ran* a line of code; mutation measures whether it *asserted hard enough to catch a behavioral change*. High coverage with weak assertions passes coverage and fails mutation.

### Mutation-survivor classification

The 9 mutation survivors are classified equivalent in the iteration commits that introduced them:

- Memoize cache wrapping is opaque from the public API; the wrapper layer is a private contract.
- `sort()` is per-conversion-stable; the sort comparator's tie-breaking branch isn't observable from outside.
- Optional-chain null guards are redundant with downstream `TypeError`s; killing the guard doesn't change behavior because the downstream throw catches it.

No `// Stryker disable` comments currently live in source; each classification rides on its commit. If a future PR drops the score below the gate, CI blocks the merge until the surviving mutants are killed or explicitly classified with a Stryker-disable comment explaining why.

## Release signals

Semantic-release filters `(demo)`-scoped commits from release analysis (`.releaserc.json`). Commits touching `src/` MUST NOT use the `(demo)` scope; use `(api)`, `(lib)`, or no scope. Breaking lib-API changes need `feat(api)!:` on the subject or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer in the body; a prose-only marker commit documenting a bundled change does not trigger a release. PR titles do not drive semantic-release; individual commit subjects on main do. See [EXTENDING.md](./EXTENDING.md) for the full ruleset and the commit-subject reference table.

## Key files

- [README.md](./README.md): primary docs (audience: humans evaluating the library)
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): agent and contributor orientation
- [EXTENDING.md](./EXTENDING.md): how to add a kit, add a section to an existing kit, or add a dimension; lib-side and demo-side rituals plus the tree-shake foot-gun
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md): setup, commands, commit conventions
- [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md): security policy and reporting
- [NOTICE.md](./NOTICE.md): third-party attributions (NIST, BIPM, ISO, USP, ISMP citations; nominative trademark notices; demo-dependency licenses)
